verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine that circulates as a latent complex (LLC/SLC) and can associate stably with the cell surface via LTBP or GARP (LRRC32) anchoring on regulatory T cells, platelets, and other cell types. When tethered through GARP or LTBP to transmembrane proteins, the latent TGFB1 complex is wash-resistant and accessible from the extracellular face. However, this surface presentation is cell-type restricted and not the baseline state for all cells. In the absence of such anchoring, TGFB1 is effectively secreted/soluble. Hence contextual, with tissue/cell-type-restricted stable surface attachment.
